assumptions: No Freshworks developer account, organization URL, product account, product selection, trial or sponsored subscription, app type, Node.js installation, Freshworks CLI, app directory, organization domain, account URL, or prior app is assumed.
A supported development computer, browser, terminal, and email or Google account are available.
No signup, provider authorization, verification, product account creation, trial activation, software installation, app generation, local server, or product login was performed during research.

developer_goal: Create a unified Freshworks developer account, select and create a Freshworks product trial, install the Freshworks CLI, generate the common starter app, run its local test server, and verify the app is rendered inside the selected product.

first_success.outcome: The intended terminal is the `common-starter-template` app running through the local FDK server and rendered inside the selected Freshworks product account after `?dev=true` is appended to the account URL.
first_success.observable_signal: At the app's intended product location, the generated app is visibly rendered and its function can be tested; successful Marketplace publication is a later stage.

route_selection.surface: Freshworks developer platform first app through local product rendering
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Freshworks developer platform first app through local product rendering
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Marketplace deployment
candidates.reason: Marketplace deployment follows building and testing; local rendering is the first documented working-app terminal and avoids publication and review requirements.

candidates.name: Freddy AI Copilot for Developers
candidates.reason: The Create your first app page labels Copilot as an alternative, while the main route uses the Freshworks CLI and common starter template.

candidates.name: Serverless first app
candidates.reason: Front-end and serverless are peer tabs; the rendering terminal is written for an app deployed at a product location, but no product or app type is selected before the blocked account path.

required_path.action: Complete Freshworks account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Freshworks account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Freshworks candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: At the app's intended product location, the generated app is visibly rendered and its function can be tested; successful Marketplace publication is a later stage.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'Create a unified Freshworks developer account with Google or email, or onboard an existing product account with its Product Account or Organization URL.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S7']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'verification', 'requirement': 'For email registration, follow the verification email to activate the developer account.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'configuration', 'requirement': 'Select the Freshworks product for the app, then name and create that product account.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2', 'S3']}

external_gates.description: Google and email are peer registration methods with different external and verification steps.
Email signup requires activation through a verification email.
The developer must select a Freshworks product and name its account before building an app for that host.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Test Your First App and verify that the app is rendered in its intended Freshworks product location.

excluded.item: Understanding generated app internals, adding production features, test coverage for Marketplace review, packing, validation, Marketplace submission, review, publication, monetization, production inst
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Freshworks show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
